---
title: "Research"
---


## Peer-Reviewed Articles

 - Parameswaran, G., __Sekeres, G.__, and Goldblatt, H. "The Politics of the Slippery Slope." R & R at the _Journal of Politics_.

 - Dixit, A., __Sekeres, G.__, Mariano, E., Memtsoudis, S., and Sun, E. "Association of Patient Characteristics and Hospital Choice with Utilization of Regional Anesthesia in Total Knee Arthroplasty: A Retrospective Analysis Using Medicare Claims." R & R at _Anesthesiology_.

 - __Sekeres, G.__, Miller, T., Mariano, E., Glance, L., and Sun, E. "Correlation Between Anesthesia Group Size and Merit-Based Incentive Payment System (MIPS) Scores." R & R at _Anesthesiology_.

## Senior Thesis

 - __Sekeres, G.__, [The Effects of Communication on a Middle Equilibrium in Prisoner's Dilemma](/gabe_sekeres_thesis.pdf). 2022.
 
 
## In Progress

 - Prosecutorial Discretion and Plea Bargains Under Incomplete Information: I'm modeling plea bargains in the case where the prosecutor does not have to reveal evidence to the defendant (common across the US for most evidence, localized to certain states for _Brady_ evidence, which is called a 'closed-file regime'). This is a play on Bebchuk (1984) and Silviera (2017), and I think adding a two-sided information disparity based on the same underlying distribution adds nuance to their models which better fits the observed.
 
 - Cross-time Empirical Revealed Preferences: This is very much in the conception stage, but I think there's something to the idea of implied continuity of preferences over time (_i.e._, continuously changing preferences derived from discrete observations of preferences). I'm doing PhD apps this semester, though, so this is on the back burner.
